title: DMARC Generator
helpOutput:
Usage: dmarc-generator [<options>] <domain>

  Generate a DMARC record from policy settings.

Options:
  --json               Output results in JSON format
  -s, --server=<text>  DNS server to query (default: system resolver)
  -t, --timeout=<int>  Query timeout in seconds
  -p, --policy=<text>  DMARC policy: none, quarantine, reject
  --sp=<text>          Subdomain policy: none, quarantine, reject
  --pct=<int>          Percentage of messages to apply policy to (0-100)
  --rua=<text>         Aggregate report URI (e.g., mailto:dmarc@example.com)
  --ruf=<text>         Forensic report URI (e.g., mailto:forensic@example.com)
  --adkim=<text>       DKIM alignment: relaxed (r) or strict (s)
  --aspf=<text>        SPF alignment: relaxed (r) or strict (s)
  --ri=<int>           Report interval in seconds
  --fo=<text>          Failure reporting options: 0, 1, d, s
  -h, --help           Show this message and exit

Arguments:
  <domain>  Domain name the DMARC record is for
output:
{
  "Domain": "example.com",
  "DMARC Record": "v\u003dDMARC1; p\u003dnone",
  "DNS Name": "_dmarc.example.com",
  "Policy": "none (monitor only)",
  "DKIM Alignment": "relaxed",
  "SPF Alignment": "relaxed",
  "Percentage": "100%",
  "Aggregate Reports": "not configured",
  "Forensic Reports": "not configured"
}

DNS TXT Record:
_dmarc.example.com  IN  TXT  "v=DMARC1; p=none"
